category_title: Young children with anxiety exhibiting internalizing 'Freeze' or 'Flight' behaviors
minimal_context: The student is finishing a 60-minute 'Build-to-Play' session and needs to pack up to transition to a new activity.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Our 60 minutes of play is over, so it is time to leave the session.

STUDENT: (Goes into a shell, becoming quiet and withdrawn instead of packing up.)

TUTOR: Let's get your things together so we can go.

STUDENT: (Stays back and watches, not making eye contact with anyone.)

TUTOR: We need to transition to the next activity right now.

STUDENT: (Starts crying, not communicating.) Whenever it's time to leave I want to cry.

options.A: Position the facilitator physically away from the student, providing support only intermittently rather than constantly during the transition out of the session.
options.B: End the session by summarizing everyone's effort using the visual chart of tokens, having the children rate the materials, and providing a home mission sheet.
options.C: Allow the student to withdraw from the transition process, letting them remain quiet and withdrawn at the end of the Build-to-Play session.
options.D: Give the student a direct warning regarding their crying behavior before proceeding with the transition out of the Build-to-Play session.
